summary.en: The sprawling, creative heart of modern Nashville, known for its laid-back, artistic vibe and an undeniable concentration of the city's most innovative and exciting food.
A gritty, creative, and rapidly evolving former industrial district where old warehouses now house art galleries, distilleries, and ambitious restaurants.
One of Nashville's oldest neighborhoods, defined by its Victorian architecture, brick sidewalks, and a quiet, residential feel that hosts upscale dining and funky corner bars.
A sleek, modern, and upscale LEED-certified neighborhood known for high-rises and trendy shops, but also home to legendary Nashville traditions.
A quiet, charming, and primarily residential neighborhood that rewards explorers with tucked-away gems and incredible food, feeling like a small town within the city.
A curated look at downtown beyond the tourist traps, focusing on a historic shopping arcade and a modern social club that locals actually frequent.

description.en: An airy, all-day café with a beautiful marble-topped bar, perfect for a morning coffee or a cheeky lunchtime cocktail.
A unique outdoor venue hosting a rotating selection of wine, cocktails, and pop-up food concepts; a true 'in-the-know' local hangout.
An intimate spot specializing in Iberian food with a fantastic drink menu, perfect for lingering with a glass of Spanish wine.
A casual, walk-in-only Japanese lunch spot that earned a James Beard nomination. The onigiri is phenomenal, but expect a queue.
A legendary, no-frills taco joint famous for its chicken tortilla soup. Remember to bring cash.
An acclaimed Italian-inspired restaurant where the clam pizza is a must-order, showcasing a perfect balance of creativity and classic technique.
This Chinese-fusion spot is housed in a distinctive, retro-looking building that exemplifies the neighborhood's quirky architecture and adaptive reuse.
A buzzy, open-air space that’s a local favorite for brunch or dinner, embodying the social, communal feel of East Nashville dining.
A destination in itself, this casual cocktail bar has an inventive drink menu and a relaxed, industrial vibe that makes it a very cool spot.
This fresh pasta paradise has a thoughtfully curated wine list, making it the perfect place for a glass of Italian wine.
A new, high-quality raw fish and sushi spot from a respected local restaurant group, adding to the neighborhood's reputation for culinary excellence.
Wander off the main drag to see the surviving brick warehouses, loading docks, and smokestacks that define We-Ho's raw, industrial aesthetic.
An airy, elevated barroom and restaurant with a fantastic raw bar and seasonal, Southern-inspired shareable plates.
A funky, 'come-as-you-are' riverfront bar that feels a world away from downtown. A genuine local hangout with one of the best burgers in town.
A true hidden gem in an unassuming house serving classic Southern breakfast and lunch with legendary hospitality. The jerk chicken on Thursdays is a must.
A reliable and stylish spot for when you want solid, high-quality sushi in a sophisticated setting.
Stroll down 4th Ave North and surrounding streets to admire the preserved Victorian-era homes and buildings that give Germantown its historic character.
A Nashville institution serving Memphis-style dry-rub ribs and what many call the city's best pulled pork sandwich. A must-visit for authentic Southern BBQ.
For a sophisticated drink, head to this bar at the W Hotel for Mediterranean-inspired cocktails in a chic, modern setting.
What began as a food truck is now a fantastic restaurant swapping casual fare for elevated ceviches and decadent moles.
At The Catbird Seat and Maiz De La Vida, the food presentation is a work of art. Part of the experience is admiring the craft on the plate.
Observe the striking contrast between the old, low-slung brick building of Peg Leg Porker and the gleaming new high-rises that surround it.
A neighborhood Tex-Mex spot known for its fun happy hour. The 'teeny 'tinis' are a local favorite.
A beloved, long-standing neighborhood institution serving American classics done right. The fried green tomato BLT is a standout.
The true spirit of Sylvan Park is discovery. Walk the main streets to find the unassuming storefronts of hidden gems like Kardes and Korea House.
Just west of the main commercial drag, this paved trail offers a beautiful, peaceful walk along the creek, showing the neighborhood's natural side.
A multi-faceted social club with a fantastic coffee shop, cocktail bar, restaurant, bowling alley, and dipping pools, offering an all-in-one urban escape.
A burger joint inside The Arcade where the main attraction isn't the smash patties, but the perfectly crispy fries.

prompt.en: Find a piece of street art or a colorful mural that captures the artistic spirit of East Nashville and take a photo.
Take a picture of a storefront that looks fiercely independent and unique. What makes it stand out?
Capture a delicious-looking dish or drink from a local spot. Focus on the colors and textures.
Find a spot where a quiet residential street meets a vibrant commercial strip. Photograph the contrast.
Photograph an interesting or unusual building. Look for unique shapes, materials, or signs of adaptive reuse.
Take a selfie on a patio or outdoor space where locals are hanging out.
Record a 10-second audio clip of the neighborhood's ambient sound. Is it music, chatter, or quiet streets?
Find a product in a local shop that is made by a Nashville artisan and take a photo of it.
Capture a scene that perfectly illustrates the 'laid-back' vibe of the neighborhood.
Spot a food truck or a pop-up food stall and snap a picture of its menu or logo.
Find a small park or green space tucked between the houses and shops. Take a photo of this urban oasis.
As evening approaches, capture the warm glow from a restaurant or bar window.
Photograph a detail—like a unique house number, a garden gnome, or a quirky mailbox—that shows local personality.
Take a photo of your coffee cup against the backdrop of a cool local cafe.
Take a selfie in front of a building that you think best represents the creative energy of East Nashville.
Find a textured surface like weathered brick, rusted metal, or cracked concrete and take a close-up photo.
Capture a photo that shows the contrast between an old industrial building and a modern piece of art or a new business.
Photograph an example of how an old industrial feature (like a loading dock or a large window) has been repurposed for a new use.
Take a selfie in front of a building you think is an art gallery or creative studio.
Play with the light and shadows cast by the large warehouse buildings. Create a dramatic architectural photo.
Find a sign or menu for an ambitious restaurant or cocktail bar and take a photo of it.
Find a remnant of the neighborhood's industrial past, like old train tracks, a smokestack, or faded lettering on a brick wall.
Take a photo that includes both a construction site and a historic building, showing the neighborhood's rapid evolution.
Amidst the brick and concrete, find a surprising pop of color—a painted door, a mural, or a bright sign—and make it the focus of your photo.
Many distilleries are in this area. Find a sign or symbol related to spirits (like a barrel or a brand logo) and snap a pic.
Take a photo of your shoes against the backdrop of the industrial ground—asphalt, gravel, or old cobblestones.
Take a wide-angle shot of a massive warehouse to capture its impressive scale.
Find an element that feels raw (like a rusty pipe) next to something that feels refined (like a fancy light fixture) and photograph them together.
Many spots here have unassuming entrances. Find a doorway that looks mysterious or hidden and take a picture.
Take a selfie with a giant warehouse or factory smokestack in the background.
Find an ornate architectural detail on a historic Victorian home—like gingerbread trim, a stained-glass window, or iron fencing—and take a close-up photo.
Take a photo looking down at the neighborhood's characteristic brick sidewalks. Frame your feet in the shot!
Look up and capture a photo of the canopy of mature trees that line the residential streets.
Take a selfie on a quiet, tree-lined street with beautiful historic homes in the background.
Find a business that looks like it has been here for a very long time and photograph its storefront.
Capture a peaceful scene that embodies the quiet, residential feel of Germantown.
Photograph the elegant exterior or sign of an upscale dining establishment.
Many historic homes here have beautiful front porches. Find one with inviting chairs or a porch swing and take a picture.
Find a restaurant or bar tucked away in an unassuming building that looks like it could be a house.
Spot a bar that looks like a true 'come-as-you-are' local hangout and snap a photo of its exterior.
Find a historic-style street lamp and take an artistic photo of it, especially as dusk settles.
Capture the atmosphere of a charming outdoor patio at a local restaurant or bar.
Find a brightly painted front door on one of the historic houses and make it the subject of your photo.
Look for an official historic marker or plaque on a building and take a photo of it.
Take a selfie that captures both the historic architecture and the lush greenery of the neighborhood.
Find a modern, glass-covered high-rise and capture the reflection of the sky or an older building in its windows.
Take a photo that embodies the 'sleek, modern, and upscale' vibe of The Gulch. Think clean lines and luxury.
Find a view that captures both a traditional, old-school Nashville spot and a new, modern high-rise in the same frame.
Take a selfie from below, looking up at one of the towering condo or hotel buildings.
Photograph an interesting or stylish window display at one of the trendy shops.
Even if you're not eating there, find a restaurant known for its presentation and snap a photo of its stylish exterior.
Find a spot that feels authentically 'old Nashville' amidst the modern development and capture its character.
The Gulch is a LEED-certified neighborhood. Find a sign or feature that hints at its sustainable design (like a bike rack or green space).
The neighborhood is named for the historic train tracks. Find a vantage point where you can see the tracks and take a photo.
Find a bar that looks sophisticated and modern. Take a picture of its sign or entrance.
Stand in the middle of a street (safely!) and take a photo looking up at the high-rises on both sides, creating an 'urban canyon' effect.
Find a sign or menu advertising classic Southern food like BBQ or ribs.
Photograph the grand entrance or lobby of a boutique hotel or luxury apartment building.
The Gulch has several murals and public art installations. Find one and get creative with your photo.
Take a selfie that captures the polished, modern aesthetic of The Gulch.
Find a shop or restaurant with an unassuming facade that looks like it could be hiding something amazing inside. Take a photo of its entrance.
Capture a wide shot of a main street that shows the mix of small shops, houses, and trees, encapsulating the neighborhood's vibe.
Take a photo of a quiet, tree-lined residential street. Try to capture the peaceful atmosphere.
Take a selfie in front of a classic neighborhood institution that looks like it's been there for decades.
Find a spot on the Richland Creek Greenway or another green space and take a photo of the natural scenery.
Photograph a menu or sign advertising a unique or authentic dish, like Turkish, Korean, or Southern comfort food.
Your mission is to find the most normal-looking building that houses a highly-rated restaurant. Snap a picture.
Find a charming detail on a house, like a well-tended garden, a welcoming front porch, or a unique architectural feature.
Record a 10-second audio clip. How quiet is this neighborhood compared to others?
Find a cafe or bar that looks like a comfortable, no-fuss spot for locals. Capture its exterior.
Take a photo of the sidewalk or walking path, emphasizing the pleasant, walkable nature of the area.
Find the smallest restaurant you can and take a photo. Small spaces often have the biggest flavors.
Capture how the late afternoon sun filters through the trees onto the streets and houses.
Spot a sign advertising a happy hour special and take a picture.
Take a selfie that says 'I just discovered a wonderful secret spot'.
Capture the symmetry and architectural lines inside the historic, glass-roofed Arcade.
Find an old, historic building nestled amongst modern skyscrapers. Photograph the contrast.
Find a place that feels like an escape from the tourist crowds—a quiet alley, a cool lobby, or a local-focused shop.
Take a selfie in a spot that feels like a relaxing oasis in the middle of the bustling city.
Photograph a building from 1902 or another piece of early 20th-century architecture.
Take a photo looking down a street that is NOT Lower Broadway, capturing the vibe of the 'other' downtown.
Find a place that combines multiple activities (like food, drinks, and games) and capture its multifaceted energy.
Spot a menu that offers a modern or 'elevated' take on a classic Southern dish.
Find an element that feels retro or nostalgic, like a bowling alley or an old-school shop sign.
Find a quick, no-fuss food spot that looks perfect for a lunch break and photograph its storefront.
Take an abstract photo focusing on the geometric patterns of a modern downtown building.
Stand at the base of a tall building and take a photo looking straight up to the sky.
Find a cocktail menu with creative, unusual ingredients and take a picture of the most interesting drink name.
Inside The Arcade, photograph an old, traditional shop next to a newer, more modern one.
Take a selfie inside The Arcade, capturing its unique architecture in the background.
